mechanism_narrative: A persistently near-zero federal funds rate (currently 0.16%) reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and lowers the opportunity cost of holding risk assets versus cash, producing a structural positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as rates remain suppressed.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16 near zero lower bound
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods of FEDFUNDS below 0.5% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window while rates remain near zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate of at least 0.3 percentage points over three months from a cyclical peak signals a labor market inflection point that leads equity investors to price in an economic recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months from a local peak
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.9 percent, elevated post-shock level suggesting potential peak-and-decline pattern
failure_condition: In more than 40% of historical instances where UNRATE declines >0.3pp from peak, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production and retail sales rise simultaneously, it signals a synchronized recovery in both supply-side output and end-demand consumption, reinforcing confidence in a broad-based economic rebound and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month; the effect is materially weaker when only one series improves.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.15 and RSAFS at 387123 in post-shock recovery snapshot
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO+RSAFS positive momentum periods do not outperform single-series-positive periods in mean 20-day forward return by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the joint signal is not higher than either univariate signal.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime where unemployment remains elevated (near 8.9%) and the Fed holds rates near zero, further reductions or sustained near-zero FEDFUNDS interact with improving labor conditions (falling UNRATE) to amplify risk-asset re-rating, producing stronger positive S&P 500 returns than either signal alone over a full quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 0.16 with UNRATE 8.9 indicating post-shock zero-rate recovery regime
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with near-zero policy rates
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns conditional on FEDFUNDS near zero AND declining UNRATE are not significantly higher than returns conditional on FEDFUNDS near zero alone (p > 0.05), or the interaction effect reverses sign in more than one historical recovery episode.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in the Industrial Production Index reflects improving manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which propagates quickly into short-horizon equity sentiment, producing a positive S&P 500 return over the following trading week whenever INDPRO momentum is positive.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant difference (p > 0.05) between positive and negative INDPRO momentum periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of Housing Starts (HOUST) above its trailing 6-month average signals renewed consumer confidence and forward-looking demand for durable investment, which historically leads broader equity market gains over the following month as the housing sector is a leading indicator of the business cycle.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its trailing 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 596.0 thousand units in recovery snapshot
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward SPX return following HOUST crossing above its 6-month average is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll growth accelerates while CPI inflation remains subdued, the combination signals a benign recovery without imminent monetary tightening pressure, allowing equity markets to price in extended economic expansion and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; if payroll growth accelerates alongside rising inflation, the signal is negated as tightening expectations offset the growth benefit.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130515 thousand and CPIAUCSL at 221.062 in snapshot
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth combined with low CPIAUCSL growth does not produce significantly higher 60-day forward returns than positive PAYEMS growth combined with high CPIAUCSL growth (p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: If year-over-year CPI inflation falls below 1.5% while the federal funds rate is already near zero, markets price in deflationary risk and diminished monetary policy space to counteract further shocks, triggering a short-horizon negative reaction in S&P 500 returns as risk premia rise.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 221.062 with FEDFUNDS at zero lower bound implying limited policy space against disinflation
regime_hint: zero lower bound policy constraint regime
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following sub-1.5% CPI YoY readings under near-zero FEDFUNDS are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one historical low-inflation episode.
